description: Fanning out work via synchronous invocations that multiply into the account concurrency limit, throttling unrelated functions.
body: Failure mode: a function synchronously invokes many child functions (or a burst triggers thousands of instances), consuming the account/function concurrency ceiling and throttling every function sharing it, including unrelated workloads. Detection signals: throttling errors across multiple functions during a burst; a downstream database overwhelmed by sudden parallelism; cascading timeouts. Do not synchronously fan out into shared concurrency. Safer fallback: publish to a queue/topic and let consumers scale with reserved concurrency and batching, apply backpressure, and protect downstreams with concurrency limits and a DLQ.
